events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the compliance result directly to the required JSON file. The observed missed stored energy source is a lockout/tagout failure under OSHA 1910.147 because hazardous energy was not fully isolated, blocked, or relieved before work.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.95,
  \"event_time\": 0,
  \"rate\": 0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Lockout/tagout did not account for a stored hazardous energy source such as hydraulic pressure, gravity load, or capacitor charge.\",
    \"Equipment is not in a verified zero-energy state before continuation of work.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop affected work immediately and keep personnel clear of the hazardous energy exposure.\",
    \"Revise and re-perform the energy control procedure to identify, isolate, block, bleed, discharge, or restrain the missed stored energy source.\",
    \"Verify zero energy before resuming work and document the corrected lockout/tagout inspection.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Fail: OSHA 1910.147 requires control of all hazardous energy, including stored or residual energy, before servicing or maintenance proceeds. A missed hydraulic, gravity, or capacitor energy source can unexpectedly release energy and presents a severe injury or fatality hazard.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(c)(4) energy control procedure\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(5) stored or residual energy\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(6) verification of isolation\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
python3 -m json.tool /app/answer.json >/dev/null && cat /app/answer.json"
→ /app
